title: Withdraw 4,969,231 ada for Cardano Enterprise Adoption: Ticketing Platform

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "Phase 1 of the ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet with on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, stated as approximately $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, including a 3% Intersect administration fee." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide independent evidence for: Sellout funded Phase 1 entirely from its own capital with over $130,000 invested; Provide independent evidence for: Sellout is described as having 200,000+ registered users, 250,000+ tickets issued to date, and 350+ events annually at current cadence. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score -0.12; confidence 75.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Directional NO is supported by affirmative independent evidence of waste, duplication, excessive cost, or failed delivery — not by missing information.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: Sellout funded Phase 1 entirely from its own capital with over $130,000 invested.
Provide independent evidence for: Sellout is described as having 200,000+ registered users, 250,000+ tickets issued to date, and 350+ events annually at current cadence.
Provide independent evidence for: The proposal states that Anvil and Sellout have not received ada from the Cardano Treasury within the last 24 months.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Withdraw treasury funds for Phase 2 of a Cardano-native ticketing platform operated by Sellout.io and built by Anvil Development Agency.

Deliverables: secondary marketplace with on-chain settlement, smart-contract royalty enforcement, per-event anti-scalping controls, wallet onboarding for Sellout users, organizer tools, independent third-party security audit, public launch campaign and trade-show activations, public metrics dashboard and quarterly reporting

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Phase 1 of the ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet with on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Sellout funded Phase 1 entirely from its own capital with over $130,000 invested.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, stated as approximately $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, including a 3% Intersect administration fee.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Phase 2 is planned to deliver a secondary marketplace, royalty enforcement, anti-scalping controls, wallet onboarding, organizer tools, an independent audit, and launch marketing.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The team commits to repay $1,093,231 to the Cardano Treasury through a revenue-share mechanism, with 25% of fees paid until reimbursement and 5% thereafter.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Sellout is described as having 200,000+ registered users, 250,000+ tickets issued to date, and 350+ events annually at current cadence.
missing: Independent evidence for: Phase 1 of the ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet with on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification.
Independent evidence for: Sellout funded Phase 1 entirely from its own capital with over $130,000 invested.
Independent evidence for: Sellout is described as having 200,000+ registered users, 250,000+ tickets issued to date, and 350+ events annually at current cadence.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states that Anvil and Sellout have not received ada from the Cardano Treasury within the last 24 months.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, stated as approximately $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, including a 3% Intersect administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Phase 1 of the ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet with on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

missing: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
